channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: ICC Investigation and Duterte's Legal Challenges Continue
item.description: A recent report has clarified that there is no United Nations plan to rescue former Philippine President Rodrigo Duterte from the International Criminal Court (ICC) custody. This clarification follows...

item.title: Archbishop Makgoba Urges Action on Rampant Online Gambling in South Africa
item.description: Archbishop Thabo Makgoba has issued a stark warning about the destructive impact of online gambling in South Africa, calling for either a complete ban or strict regulations. The Archbishop’s concerns ...

item.title: Canberra Raiders in talks to extend Jed Stuart’s contract through 2029
item.description: Canberra Raiders winger Jed Stuart is close to securing a new three-year contract extension that would keep him with the NRL club until at least the end of the 2029 season. The young back, currently s...

item.title: Union Government Increases Daily Supply of 5kg LPG Cylinders for Migrant Workers Nationwide
item.description: The Union Government on Monday announced a significant increase in the allocation of 5 kg LPG cylinders for migrant labourers across India, covering all states and Union Territories. These small cylin...

item.title: Genoa Design Builds on Innovation, Community, and Employee Growth
item.description: Genoa Design International Ltd., a leading firm in detail design and 3D modelling for shipbuilding, has grown from kitchen-table beginnings in Newfoundland 30 years ago to international operations in ...

item.title: Affordable Regional Victorian Towns for Family Homes Under $500,000
item.description: As property prices in Melbourne surge, regional Victoria offers some of the last opportunities for buyers with a $500,000 budget to secure a freestanding family home. Towns like Churchill in the Latro...

item.title: Federal government surveys Canadians on brightness of modern car headlights
item.description: Transport Canada has launched a national survey to gather feedback from drivers about whether the headlights on modern vehicles are too bright, amid growing complaints from motorists who say they expe...

item.title: Transcona Roofing Supports Career Growth and Employee Development
item.description: Transcona Roofing Ltd., a commercial roofing and waterproofing company in Winnipeg, demonstrates a strong commitment to employee career development. Field supervisor Perry French, who faced physical c...

item.title: NEMA Under Scrutiny Over Missing Environmental Records for Northern Energy Projects
item.description: Fresh revelations from the National Environment Management Authority (NEMA) have sparked questions about environmental compliance among major energy projects in northern Kenya. According to records su...

item.title: High-Protein Diets Show Potential to Significantly Reduce Cholera Infections
item.description: A recent study by the University of California, Riverside suggests that diet could play a crucial role in preventing cholera, a severe diarrheal disease. Researchers found that diets rich in specific ...

item.title: Madrid and Basque leaders dispute over relocation of Picasso’s Guernica for 90th anniversary
item.description: Tensions have flared between the Madrid and Basque regional governments over a proposal to temporarily move Pablo Picasso’s iconic mural, Guernica, from Madrid’s Reina Sofía Museum to Bilbao’s Guggenh...

item.title: Tanzania boosts higher education with record 3.6 trillion shillings in student loans since 2021
item.description: Over the past five years under President Samia Suluhu Hassan’s leadership, Tanzania’s Higher Education Students’ Loans Board (HESLB) has made remarkable progress by disbursing a total of 3.6 trillion ...

item.title: Bomb Explosion in Manipur Claims Lives of Two Children, Mother Injured
item.description: In a tragic incident in Manipur's Bishnupur district, two children, aged five months and five years, lost their lives in a bomb attack at Tronglaobi Awang Leikai. The mother of the children was also i...

item.title: Andrew Hastie Challenges Liberal Party Orthodoxy and Explores Progressive Tax Ideas
item.description: Andrew Hastie, recently reinstated as a Liberal frontbencher, has been stirring debate within his party by publicly questioning its traditional stances. Known for his socially conservative views, Hast...

item.title: LAUSD Unions Set to Strike on April 14, Disrupting Schools and Childcare Plans
item.description: Los Angeles Unified School District (LAUSD) faces a potential historic strike beginning April 14, 2026, involving its three major unions: UTLA (teachers and counselors), Local 99 (support staff), and ...

item.title: Petrol Pump Owners and Dealers Urge Govt for Commission Hike Amid Rising Fuel Prices
item.description: Petrol pump owners and dealers in Pakistan have raised alarms over the increasing operational costs due to the recent surge in fuel prices. The All Pakistan Petrol Pump Owners Association (APPPOA) and...

item.title: Australia’s EV Charging Blackspots and the Need for Better Infrastructure
item.description: As electric vehicle (EV) sales rise in Australia, concerns around access to charging stations have become more prominent. While urban areas are generally well-served with charging options, rural and r...

item.title: A look at Satchel Paige’s seven children and their lives
item.description: Satchel Paige, one of baseball’s legendary pitchers, became a father relatively late in life, fathering seven children with his second wife, Lahoma Jean Brown. His first marriage produced no children....

item.title: What to do if your UTI symptoms persist or keep returning
item.description: Urinary Tract Infections (UTIs) are common, especially among women, and often cause discomfort like urgency, frequent urination, and burning sensations. In a bid to streamline care, women can now acce...

item.title: Smith Bros. & Wilson fosters a supportive and inclusive workplace culture
item.description: Smith Bros. & Wilson, a Surrey-based construction company with a history spanning over 125 years, is recognised for its employee-focused culture. Unlike larger companies where employees often feel lik...
